Org_FormalName: National Park Service Water Resources Division
Location_Name: Arkansas River at Bent's Old Fort National Historic Site
Location_Description: Arkansas River: The segment of the Arkansas River that flows through Bent's Old Fort (COARLA01b_A) is characterized as having water quality impairments for Total Arsenic, Dissolved Selenium and Manganese, and Temperature). Arsenic, E. coli, selenium and manganese are the most common listings for rivers and streams in this region. This index reach is largely characterized by shallow glides and run habitats with sandy substrates. Instream vegetation is sparse, and willows line the banks with cottonwoods common on the floodplains south of the active river channel. Designated uses for the Arkansas River include Agriculture, Aquatic Life Warm Class 2, Recreation (Existing Primary Contact Use), Domestic Water Supply, Water + Fish. The assessment unit is COARLA01b 1b and includes the mainstem of the Arkansas River from the Colorado Canal headgate to the inlet to John Martin Reservoir.

Org_FormalName: Colorado River Watch (Volunteer)
Location_Description: From Beulah MS within the town of Beulah, travel east 3 mi on state Hwy 76 to Rd 3R; turn south and continue 1/2 mi to bridge.  Samples are taken on private property, 300 yds. upstream from bridge. (Hop over fence to get to stream.)

Org_FormalName: Colorado River Watch (Volunteer)
Location_Name: Fountain Cr - P1 - Abv Conf Ark
Location_Description: Fr Pueblo, take I-25 S.  Take Exit 99A toward 6th St and turn L onto Albany Ave.  Go L on E 4th St, then R on S Joplin Ave, then R on S La Crosse.  Drive 1/4 mi to parking lot on L.  Follow path to R, then L near gauge station to site.

Org_FormalName: Colorado River Watch (Volunteer)
Colorado River Watch (Volunteer)
Location_Name: South Cr - Blw Conf Squirrel Cr
Location_Description: Just after entering the Beulah valley from the East (Hwy 78), the road forks into Pine Dr and Grand Ave.  Just below the 1st bridge on Grand is a slope that leads to the site.

Org_FormalName: Colorado River Watch (Volunteer)
Location_Description: Driving directions:  From I-25, at south end of Pueblo, exit on Pueblo Blvd, go west to Northern Ave, about 3 miles, go west on Northern, also known as HWY 78, to Beulah, about 21 miles.  at fork, stay rightt, go through downtown Beulah, you are now on Grand Ave.  Go about 1/2 mile past the Beulah Inn, you will see a bridge to the right, do not go over the bridge, instead stay west and go up the dirt road.  Road forks:  Pine Ave to left, Cascade to the right.  Take Cascade.  You are passing a green house enclosed by wrought iron fence.  There is a drive to the right, dirt, over a private bridge, address is 9011 Cascade.  About 1/2 way up drive is testing site of Middle Creek.  Private property, owned by Craig Bow.

Org_FormalName: Colorado River Watch (Volunteer)
Location_Description: From town of Las Animas, travel approx 2.25 mi south on state hwy 101 to bridge over Purgatoire River.

Org_FormalName: Colorado River Watch (Volunteer)
Location_Description: From town limit of Las Animas, travel approx 1/2 mi north on US Hwy 50 to bridge over Arkansas River.

Org_FormalName: Colorado River Watch (Volunteer)
Location_Description: From I-25 and Hwy 24 in Colorado Springs, travel west on Hwy 24. Turn south (left) on 26th street to intersection of Gold Camp Road. Here 26th becomes Bear Creek Road. Continue on Bear Creek road to point where road ends. A paved road continues at an extreme left. This is Gold Camp Road. At this intersection follow gravel road south. This is High Drive. Park here.
